rules.risk: Anonymous authentication allows unauthenticated requests to the kubelet API (port 10250). An attacker on the network can exec into pods, read logs, and access the node without credentials.
rules.remediation: Set authentication.anonymous.enabled to false in the KubeletConfiguration.

rules.risk: The read-only port (default 10255) exposes kubelet metrics and pod information without authentication. An attacker can enumerate pods, resource usage, and node configuration.
rules.remediation: Set readOnlyPort to 0 in the KubeletConfiguration to disable the read-only endpoint.

rules.description: Kubelet authorization mode is set to AlwaysAllow
rules.risk: AlwaysAllow authorization permits any authenticated request to perform any operation on the kubelet API, including exec into pods and reading secrets. This bypasses all RBAC controls at the node level.
rules.remediation: Set authorization.mode to Webhook in the KubeletConfiguration so kubelet delegates authorization to the API server.

rules.risk: Without protectKernelDefaults, pods can modify kernel parameters via sysctl that may affect the host or other pods. This weakens node-level isolation.
rules.remediation: Set protectKernelDefaults to true in the KubeletConfiguration.

rules.description: Kubelet streaming connection idle timeout is set to 0 (disabled)
rules.risk: Disabling the streaming connection timeout allows exec/attach sessions to remain open indefinitely. An attacker who establishes a session will not be disconnected automatically.
rules.remediation: Set streamingConnectionIdleTimeout to a non-zero value (e.g., 5m) in the KubeletConfiguration.

rules.description: Kubelet event recording is disabled (eventRecordQPS set to 0)
rules.risk: Disabling event recording suppresses important audit events from the kubelet. Security-relevant events such as pod creation failures, image pull errors, and OOM kills will not be recorded.
rules.remediation: Set eventRecordQPS to a non-zero value (default is 5) in the KubeletConfiguration.

rules.description: Kubelet TLS certificate file is not configured
rules.risk: Without explicit TLS certificate configuration and with rotateCertificates disabled, the kubelet may serve its API over unencrypted HTTP or with a self-signed certificate, enabling man-in-the-middle attacks.
rules.remediation: Set tlsCertFile and tlsPrivateKeyFile, or enable rotateCertificates with serverTLSBootstrap in the KubeletConfiguration.

rules.description: Kubernetes daemon has no corresponding systemd service detected
rules.risk: In embedded/automotive environments the kubelet/k3s/rke2 daemon must be managed by systemd. Without a systemd service, the daemon has no supervised lifecycle management and will not auto-recover from crashes.
rules.remediation: Create a systemd service unit file for the Kubernetes daemon (kubelet/k3s/rke2).

rules.description: Kubernetes daemon systemd service does not restrict capabilities via CapabilityBoundingSet
rules.risk: Without CapabilityBoundingSet in the systemd unit, the kubelet/k3s/rke2 process inherits all capabilities. Restricting capabilities limits the blast radius if the daemon is compromised.
rules.remediation: Add 'CapabilityBoundingSet=' to the [Service] section of the kubelet/k3s/rke2 systemd unit to restrict capabilities.

rules.description: K3s server token file is readable by group or other users
rules.risk: The K3s token file (/var/lib/rancher/k3s/server/token) contains the cluster bootstrap secret. If readable by non-root users, any local user can join new nodes to the cluster or authenticate as a cluster member.
rules.remediation: Set permissions on /var/lib/rancher/k3s/server/token to 0600 (owner-only read/write). Ensure the file is owned by root:root.

rules.risk: The registries.yaml file contains plaintext passwords, tokens, or auth strings for container registries. Any user with read access to this file can pull or push images using these credentials.
rules.remediation: Use credential helpers or mounted secrets instead of embedding credentials in registries.yaml. If credentials must be in the file, restrict permissions to 0600 root:root.
